actions.name: add_call

clientUniqueId.description: A call's unique identifier in the PBX or the recording system. Gong uses this identifier to prevent repeated attempts to upload the same recording.

title.description: The title of the call. This title is available in the Gong system for indexing and search.

purpose.description: The purpose of the call. This optional field is a free text of up to 255 characters.

scheduledStart.description: The date and time the call was scheduled to begin in the ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2018-02-18T02:30:00-07:00' or '2018-02-18T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC);

scheduledEnd.description: The date and time the call was scheduled to end in the ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2018-02-18T02:30:00-07:00' or '2018-02-18T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC);

actualStart.description: The actual date and time when the call started in the ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2018-02-18T02:30:00-07:00' or '2018-02-18T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC);

duration.description: The actual call duration in seconds.

parties.description: A list of the call's participants. A party must be provided for the primaryUser.

items.description: A list of the call's participants. A party must be provided for the primaryUser.

direction.description: Whether the call is inbound (someone called the company), outbound (a rep dialed someone outside the company), or a conference call.

disposition.description: The disposition of the call. The disposition is free text of up to 255 characters.

context.description: A list of references to external systems such as CRM, Telephony System, Case Management, etc.

items.description: Links to external systems such as CRM, Telephony System, Case Management etc. Currently only Salesforce and Generic are supported.

customData.description: Optional metadata associated with the call (represented as text). Gong stores this metadata and it can be used for troubleshooting.

speakersTimeline.description: The audio recording speech segments (who spoke when). Note that speakersTimeline and mediaChannelId are mutually exclusive, when providing speakersTimeline - mediaChannelId will not be used.

meetingUrl.description: The URL of the conference call by which users join the meeting

callProviderCode.description: The code identifies the provider conferencing or telephony system. For example: zoom, clearslide, gotomeeting, ringcentral, outreach, insidesales, etc. These values are predefined by Gong, please contact help@gong.io to find the proper valu

downloadMediaUrl.description: The URL from which Gong can download the media file. The URL must be unique, the audio or video file must be a maximum of 1.5GB. The content-type must either start with 'audio' or 'video,' or should be 'application/octet-stream' or 'binary/

workspaceId.description: Optional workspace identifier. If specified, the call will be placed into this workspace, otherwise, the default algorithm for workspace placement will be applied.

languageCode.description: The language code the call should be transcribed to. This field is optional as Gong automatically detects the language spoken in the call and transcribes it accordingly. Set this field only if you are sure of the language the call is in. Va

flowContext.description: The Id for the task the call should be associated with

primaryUser.description: The Gong internal user ID of the team member who hosted the call.

actions.risk: high: sends call, engagement, or digital interaction content to Gong; requires reverse ETL approval

actions.name: create_permission_profile

id.description: Unique numeric identifier of the permission profile (up to 20 digits).

overrideScore.description: User can override someone else score.

shareCallsWithCustomers.description: User can share calls with customers.

manuallyScheduleAndUploadCalls.description: User can manually schedule and upload calls.

privateCalls.description: User can set their own calls as private.

callsAndSearch.description: User can view and search calls.

dealsInlineEditing.description: User can perform inline editing of deals.

teamStats.description: User can view team stats page.

forecastManage.description: User can manage forecast boards and upload targets.

engageManageCompanyTemplates.description: User can manage company email templates.

engageCreateAndManageRulesets.description: User can create and manage rulesets.

engageSnoozeFlowToDosForOthers.description: User can snooze flow in to dos for others

engageAllowCrmFieldsViewChange.description: User can change crm fields view

viewEngageAnalyticsActivity.description: User can view engage analytics activity page.

viewEngageAnalyticsPerformance.description: User can view engage analytics performance page.

viewEngageAnalyticsFlows.description: User can view engage analytics flows page.

manageGeneralBusinessSettings.description: User can manage general business settings.

exportCallsAndCoachingDataToCSV.description: User can export calls and coaching metrics data to CSV.

crmDataInlineEditing.description: User can perform inline editing of crm data.

crmDataImport.description: User can perform import of crm data.

engageReassignFlowToDosToOthers.description: User can reassign flow to-dos to others

engageAssignFlowToDosToOthers.description: User can assign flow to-dos to others

orchestrateCreateAndManagePlays.description: User can create and manage plays.

actions.risk: high: administrative Gong settings or permissions mutation; requires reverse ETL approval and destructive confirmation

actions.name: update_meeting

startTime.description: The meeting start time in ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2018-02-18T02:30:00-07:00' or '2018-02-18T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC).

endTime.description: The meeting end time in ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2018-02-18T02:30:00-07:00' or '2018-02-18T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC).

invitees.description: A list of email addresses of invitees to the event (not including the organizer).

items.description: Attendees the requesting party should add to the invitation, this should support adding email addresses such as coordinator@gong.io for Gong to schedule the recording of the meeting.

externalId.description: The ID as it is formed on the external system.

provider.description: The web conferencing provider to use for this meeting (e.g. 'zoom'). When not specified, the user's default provider is used.

organizerEmail.description: The email address of the user who created the meeting.

actions.risk: medium: mutates Gong API state; requires reverse ETL approval

actions.name: delete_meeting

organizerEmail.description: The email address of the user who created the meeting.

actions.risk: high: removes Gong access, integration, meeting, or flow assignment state; requires reverse ETL approval and destructive confirmation

actions.name: content_viewed

reportingSystem.description: The unique identifier of the reporting system. It is the same value in all events originating from the same system.

eventTimestamp.description: The date and time when the event happened in the ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2021-08-01T02:30:00+05:00' or '2021-08-01T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC);

eventId.description: The original id of the event as designated in the reporting system.

contentId.description: The id of the content that was viewed in the reporting system.

contentUrl.description: The url of the content that was viewed in the reporting system. This is the url that is was accessed by the viewer.

contentTitle.description: Human readable title of the content.

viewActionTitle.description: The name of the action like "Document Viewed" or "Presentation Opened".

shareId.description: The id of the share action that corresponds to this view event, in case there can be more than one share per content.

viewInfoUrl.description: The link to a page that presents additional information about this event.

crmContext.description: A list of references to external systems such as CRM, Telephony System, Case Management, etc.

items.description: Links to external systems such as CRM, Telephony System, Case Management etc. Currently only Salesforce and Generic are supported.

contentProperties.description: A list of additional properties for the content

items.description: A list of additional properties for the event

eventProperties.description: A list of additional properties for the event

items.description: A list of additional properties for the event

userAgent.description: "User-Agent" header value for browser based interaction

mobileAppId.description: The application identification string in case of interaction via mobile application (bundle identifier or package name).

agentPlatform.description: Platform on which the interaction was made

workspaceId.description: Optional workspace identifier. If specified, the event will be placed into this workspace, otherwise, the default algorithm for workspace placement will be applied.

trackingId.description: The ID used for tracking the person who did the event. Must be null if a viewer is sent instead.

actions.risk: high: sends call, engagement, or digital interaction content to Gong; requires reverse ETL approval

actions.name: content_shared

reportingSystem.description: The unique identifier of the reporting system. It is the same value in all events originating from the same system.

eventTimestamp.description: The date and time when the event happened in the ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2021-08-01T02:30:00+05:00' or '2021-08-01T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC);

eventId.description: The original id of the event as designated in the reporting system.

contentId.description: The id of the content that was shared in the reporting system.

contentUrl.description: The url of the content that was shared in the reporting system. This is the url that is was accessed by the viewer.

contentTitle.description: Human readable title of the content.

shareId.description: The id of the share action, in case there can be more than one share per content.

shareInfoUrl.description: The link to a page that presents additional information about this event.

sharingMessageSubject.description: The subject of share email / message.

sharingMessageBody.description: The share message body. Can contain HTML and will be cleaned when it is presented.

recipients.description: array of objects with name + email of recipients. Email is required.

crmContext.description: A list of references to external systems such as CRM, Telephony System, Case Management, etc.

items.description: Links to external systems such as CRM, Telephony System, Case Management etc. Currently only Salesforce and Generic are supported.

contentProperties.description: A list of additional properties for the content

items.description: A list of additional properties for the event

eventProperties.description: A list of additional properties for the event

items.description: A list of additional properties for the event

workspaceId.description: Optional workspace identifier. If specified, the event will be placed into this workspace, otherwise, the default algorithm for workspace placement will be applied.

actionName.description: The name of the action like "Document Sent" or "Presentation Shared".

actions.risk: high: sends call, engagement, or digital interaction content to Gong; requires reverse ETL approval

actions.name: create_meeting

startTime.description: The meeting start time in ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2018-02-18T02:30:00-07:00' or '2018-02-18T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC).

endTime.description: The meeting end time in ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2018-02-18T02:30:00-07:00' or '2018-02-18T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC).

invitees.description: A list of email addresses of invitees to the event (not including the organizer).

items.description: Attendees the requesting party should add to the invitation, this should support adding email addresses such as coordinator@gong.io for Gong to schedule the recording of the meeting.

externalId.description: The ID as it is formed on the external system.

provider.description: The web conferencing provider to use for this meeting (e.g. 'zoom'). When not specified, the user's default provider is used.

actions.name: add_digital_interaction

eventId.description: The provider's unique identifier for the event used for deduplication

timestamp.description: The date and time of the event in ISO 8601 format

eventType.description: The type of the event, explaining the content and action performed. Values may be partner-aligned, for example, "link clicked", "page viewed"

sourceSystemName.description: When integrating via IPaaS, the name of the technology partner the customer set up a Gong integration with via an IPaaS. When integrating directly with Gong: the name of the company setting up the integration with Gong

sessionId.description: The identifier for the session, useful for tying related events together

device.description: The device used during the event

content.description: The content the person looked at

person.description: The person who did the event. Must be null if a tracking ID is sent instead.

customFields.description: Include additional custom data about the event

items.description: Include additional custom data about the event

trackingId.description: The ID used for tracking the person who did the event. Must be null if a person is sent instead.

actions.name: update_task

userId.description: The id of the Gong user who owns the task

status.description: (Optional) Sets a new status for the task

dueDate.description: (Optional) Sets a new due date and time for the task in ISO-8601 format ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2018-02-18T02:30:00-07:00' or '2018-02-18T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC).

priority.description: (Optional) Sets a new priority for the task

actions.risk: medium: mutates Gong API state; requires reverse ETL approval
